title: Modern Development Workflow and Modules

learningObjectives.text: Explain packages and tooling using a new example and justify the result.
Explain bundling and transpilation using a new example and justify the result.
Explain modules using a new example and justify the result.
Explain imports, exports, and dependencies using a new example and justify the result.

whyThisMatters: Learners frequently use modern development workflow and modules without a clear model of why it behaves as it does; this lesson makes that model explicit. It belongs to the **Modern JavaScript Development** module.
mentalModel: Draw a decision map: **Packages and tooling** → **Bundling and transpilation** → **Modules** → **Imports, exports, and dependencies**. Add branches where the learner must choose an alternative and label the evidence for the choice.

sections.title: Packages and tooling
sections.body: Packages distribute reusable code and tools. Package managers record dependencies and scripts make workflows repeatable.

sections.title: Bundling and transpilation
sections.body: Bundlers build dependency graphs and emit deployable assets; transpilers transform syntax for target environments.

sections.title: Modules
sections.body: A module is a file-level unit with explicit imports and exports. It exposes a public surface and makes dependencies visible.

sections.title: Imports, exports, and dependencies
sections.body: Exports define a module's public API; imports declare required values. Narrow APIs and acyclic dependencies improve maintainability.

sections.title: Worked example
sections.explanation: The export defines a public API and the import makes the dependency explicit.

commonMistakes: Memorizing syntax without predicting the underlying value or execution order.
Mutating shared data when the task expects a new value or explicit ownership.
Treating browser APIs and language features as the same layer.
Choosing an abstraction from habit rather than required operations.
summary: Modern Development Workflow and Modules connects Packages and tooling, Bundling and transpilation, Modules.
Use the topic to make behavior and decisions explicit rather than memorizing isolated syntax.
Verify understanding by explaining a new example and answering the topic quiz without looking back.

topicQuiz.failurePolicy: Show explanations, add missed objectives to local review, and allow immediate or later retry.

questions.prompt: Why does this module import fail?

choices.label: Named exports can only contain numbers.
choices.feedback: This choice does not resolve the assessed issue. Re-trace the Modern Development Workflow and Modules rule and the observable result.

choices.label: Imports automatically search for similarly named globals.
choices.feedback: This choice does not resolve the assessed issue. Re-trace the Modern Development Workflow and Modules rule and the observable result.

choices.label: The module exports `mastery` as a named export but imports it as a default export, so the import does not match the export contract.
choices.feedback: The module exports `mastery` as a named export but imports it as a default export, so the import does not match the export contract.

questions.explanation: The module exports `mastery` as a named export but imports it as a default export, so the import does not match the export contract.
questions.hint: Trace the actual values, references, call form, render cycle, request flow, or server/client boundary before choosing.

choices.label: Import every module with `*` and ignore the namespace object.

choices.label: Use `import { mastery } from './progress.js'` or intentionally change the source module to a default export.
choices.feedback: Use `import { mastery } from './progress.js'` or intentionally change the source module to a default export.

choices.label: Duplicate the function in `dashboard.js`.
choices.feedback: This choice does not resolve the assessed issue. Re-trace the Modern Development Workflow and Modules rule and the observable result.

choices.label: Convert the score to a string before importing.
choices.feedback: This choice does not resolve the assessed issue. Re-trace the Modern Development Workflow and Modules rule and the observable result.

questions.explanation: Use `import { mastery } from './progress.js'` or intentionally change the source module to a default export.
questions.hint: Choose the smallest change that fixes the underlying model, not a workaround that hides the symptom.

assessmentProfile.label: Mental model plus practical code reasoning
assessmentProfile.rationale: The topic combines an important model with implementation consequences, so both explanation and code reasoning are required.
assessmentProfile.inLessonPolicy: Use concise concept checks; coding and hybrid topics include at least one code-reading check.
assessmentProfile.topicQuizPolicy: Questions Q04–Q08 form the topic quiz. The presentation mix follows this profile.
